$comment: C1 — content-pack source schema v1. Version is IN THE FILENAME (pack.v1.json); there is no in-body version field, matching packages/kernel/schemas/events.v1.json's convention exactly. A source pack is one already-parsed JS value (compile() does no file/YAML IO — see the C1 design doc, Open Questions #6): { name, version, entities[], tables[], maps[] }. name/version are authoring metadata, not hashed (see src/hash.ts). Every id is namespaced per DELTA §0.3 (entity:x, table:y, map:z). Component data (EntitySource.components, TableSource.rows, MapLegendEntrySource.components) is DELIBERATELY generic JsonValue: C1 validates structurally (well-formed JSON, resolved $refs) but not against C3's (not-yet-defined) per-component schemas — orchestrator decision #4 on the C1 design doc. This means conditions (ConditionNode) that live inside component data are NOT reachable from this schema's own $ref graph (there is no Lock/Interactable awareness here); the closed-oneOf 'no dynamic code path' proof for those in-component conditions is instead enforced by src/conditions.ts's hydrateConditions() at compile time, re-validating any object it recognizes (by shape: exactly one own key in {all,any,not,fact,cmp}) against this schema's own #/$defs/ConditionNode fragment. See src/conditions.ts for the full rationale.

ConditionNode.$comment: THE closed set. Exactly these 5 shapes, nothing else — the structural half of the 'no dynamic code path' proof. There is no escape-hatch key name (no 'expr', no 'js', no 'code') anywhere in this grammar: adding a 6th shape is the only way to add an operator, and doing so requires editing this file.

JsonValue.$comment: Recursive any-JSON-value schema, used for component/table-row/map-legend-component data C1 does not otherwise constrain (see the file-level $comment above for why).

MapLegendEntrySource.$comment: Either reference an authored template entity, or an inline component bag, or both fields absent (schema allows either/neither/both — see the C1 design doc's Open Q #2 note on why this is left loose rather than requiring exactly-one).

floor.$comment: Single reserved 'empty' legend token per map (design Open Q #7, accepted as-is). Single-character, matching every topdown-puzzle ASCII vocabulary token (# B D @ H V M E/W/N/S) confirmed by games/topdown-puzzle/legacy/src/scenes/KyeScene.js.
